title: hieracium sagittatum subsp. sarcophyllum
intro: Hieracium sagittatum subsp. sarcophyllum, also known as the Arrowhead Hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be applied topically to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and can be consumed as a tea or infused in oil for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations of Hieracium sagittatum subsp. sarcophyllum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
